$comment: Season dossier pack S02, The Protest Year (Iron Circus act). Season-scoped coverage with the controversy register: appeals, arguments, and racing inside them. Ids are stable rendezvous keys, never rename. Read-side only.

templates.headline: THE PROTEST YEAR: the paddock arrives angry
templates.deck: The tenth summer's arguments followed everyone home
templates.summary: Season two opens with grievances filed, positions defended, and a championship to settle on track.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The trucks had barely parked before the first envelope reached the stewards' office. They are calling it the protest year before a wheel has turned, and the men with the loudest voices intend to prove their points with lap times instead.
sections.context: The paddock divides between those who argue with officials and those who argue with the stopwatch. The stopwatch side is shorter staffed but better armed. {pool:paddockBuzz}
sections.close: Sixteen rounds to settle it the clean way: out front, in the open, at full speed.

templates.headline: {subject} wins[[?venue: at {venue}]], the answer no appeal can overturn
templates.deck: Settled at speed, not in the stewards' room

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} In a year of envelopes and objections, {subject} chose the oldest rebuttal in the sport: be first across the line and make the paperwork irrelevant. {pool:swCrowd}
sections.stats: {pool:rrStatLine} [[?champPosition: The board reads {ord:champPosition} for {subject}, and no protest moves it.]]

templates.headline: {subject} on the podium[[?venue: at {venue}]], helmet up, chin out
templates.deck: A result the whole paddock saw with its own eyes

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} {subject} took the podium and dared anyone to object to it. In the protest year, the cleanest results are the loudest statements. {pool:swCrowd}

templates.headline: POLE for {subject}[[?venue: at {venue}]], a number nobody can protest

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} You can protest a result, a ruling, a rival's rear wing. You cannot protest the clock. {subject} holds pole[[?venue: at {venue}]] and the number is the whole argument. {pool:swBoard}
sections.close: The race will test the number. That is what races are for.

templates.headline: {subject} leads the championship, and the objections begin
templates.deck: Top of the board in the protest year
templates.summary: {subject} took the championship lead in season two.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The board has a new leader and the paddock has a new target. {subject} heads the championship, and by sundown the first questions had been raised about everything except the driving. {pool:swBoard}
sections.close: The defense of the lead runs on the road, not in the office.

templates.headline: {subject} parks it[[?venue: at {venue}]], no appeal on this one

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} {subject} slowed, parked, and climbed out[[?venue: at {venue}]]. In the year of objections there is nothing to object to here: metal failed, plainly, in public. {pool:wrenchNote}
sections.close: The crew starts the long night. The stewards, for once, get an evening off.

templates.headline: {subject} crashes out[[?venue: at {venue}]], anger costs dear
templates.deck: The protest year's temper claims another

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Maybe it was the year, maybe it was the corner, maybe it was the argument {subject} was still having three corners later. The wall does not care about any of them. {pool:swCrowd}
sections.close: The driver walks back with the wreckage of the afternoon behind him. The paddock will talk about the why all week.
